vulnerabilities.title: SGX enclave measurement checks are disabled by default, allowing arbitrary enclaves to register as trusted provers
vulnerabilities.summary: `SgxVerifier.registerInstance` trusts `IAttestation.verifyParsedQuote` to gate who may enter the SGX prover set. In `AutomataDcapV3Attestation`, the only control that binds quotes to Taiko-approved enclave code (`_trustedUserMrEnclave` and `_trustedUserMrSigner`) is conditionally skipped unless `_checkLocalEnclaveReport` is enabled. Because the flag is false by default and deployment automation sets MR values without enabling this check, a quote from an arbitrary SGX enclave can pass and registe

description.desc: `_checkLocalEnclaveReport` defaults to `false`, and MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER allowlist checks are only executed when this toggle is enabled; otherwise attestation accepts any enclave with valid QE/PCK collateral.

vulnerabilities.impact: Breaks the SGX trust model and allows unauthorized prover admission, enabling invalid-state proofs that can lead to protocol-level theft or insolvency.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: 1. Attacker runs any SGX enclave they control and generates a valid DCAP quote with `reportData` embedding an attacker-controlled ECDSA address. 2. Attacker calls `SgxVerifier.registerInstance` with the parsed quote. 3. `AutomataDcapV3Attestation.verifyParsedQuote` returns success because cert chain/QE/TCB checks pass while local enclave measurement checks are skipped. 4. After the validity delay, attacker submits SGX proofs signed by their registered instance key for attacker-chosen transitions. 5. Invalid transitions can progress through proving/verification windows and can be finalized if not superseded in time, enabling state corruption and downstream asset theft/insolvency.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Make local enclave measurement verification mandatory for all SGX attestation acceptance paths: initialize `_checkLocalEnclaveReport` to true, remove/replace the insecure toggle with explicit secure configuration (or one-way disable prevention), and fail deployment/registration unless trusted MR allowlists are enforced.

vulnerabilities.title: Mainnet tier configuration can require unresolved `tier_sgx_zkvm` verifier, forcing guardian-only progression and prolonged fund lock
vulnerabilities.summary: `MainnetTierProvider` defines tier 300 (`TIER_SGX_ZKVM`) with verifier name `tier_sgx_zkvm` and selects it probabilistically via `getMinTier`. In `LibProving.proveBlock`, non-optimistic tiers require a non-zero verifier address; otherwise proving reverts with `L1_MISSING_VERIFIER`. The provided L1 deployment flow never deploys/registers any contract under `tier_sgx_zkvm`. As a result, blocks sampled with minTier 300 cannot be progressed by normal prover flow and depend on guardian-tier intervent

description.desc: Deployment registers `tier_sgx` and `tier_guardian` but never registers `tier_sgx_zkvm`, while `MainnetTierProvider` can set `minTier` to `TIER_SGX_ZKVM` and `LibProving` reverts when that non-optimistic verifier is unresolved.

vulnerabilities.impact: Creates a deterministic liveness failure mode for a subset of blocks, which can repeatedly delay verification/finality and lock bridged or pending assets for extended periods.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: 1. Protocol is deployed with `TIER_PROVIDER=mainnet` using `DeployOnL1`. 2. A proposer waits for a slot where `meta.minTier` becomes `TIER_SGX_ZKVM` and proposes a block. 3. SGX-ZKVM proving attempts revert because `resolve("tier_sgx_zkvm")` is zero and `LibProving` throws `L1_MISSING_VERIFIER`. 4. Verification head cannot pass that block until guardian proofs are submitted. 5. Repeating this behavior produces sustained finality delays and prolonged lock of cross-chain user assets that depend on verified roots.
vulnerabilities.remediation: When `MainnetTierProvider` is used, deploy and register a verifier under `tier_sgx_zkvm` before activation. Add deployment assertions that every non-optimistic tier returned by `getTierIds()` resolves to a non-zero verifier address.
